headline: Farewell, Ultra-Cheap Flights. What Spirit’s Demise Means for You—and Airline Stocks.
summary: Picture an excessively mobile middle-class family that does almost nothing but drive, traveling from New York City to Los Angeles and back again each week.  Spirit Airlines recently succumbed.  At the same time, the strongest airlines—including  Delta Air Lines United Airlines Holdings  and some of the rest now doing their best impressions of these two—could benefit from present turmoil and generate healthy stock returns for years.

headline: Assessing United Airlines (UAL) Valuation As Recent Share Price Moves Leave Sentiment Split
summary: United Airlines Holdings (UAL) has drawn investor attention after recent share price moves, with the stock roughly flat over the past day but showing mixed performance across the past month and the past 3 months. See our latest analysis for United Airlines Holdings. Recent moves sit against a wider reset in sentiment, with a 7 day share price return of 10.78% and a 30 day share price return of 11.66% contrasting with a 90 day share price return of 13.98%. Multi year total shareholder returns...

headline: With Spirit gone, what could this mean for passenger throughput numbers?
summary: Investing.com -- Transportation Security Administration (TSA) screening data for U.S. airlines showed a marginal improvement last week, although year-over-year growth remains in negative territory.
